title: Samosa

seo.title: Authentic Pakistani Samosa Recipe - Easy & Crispy!
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of making crispy, flavorful Samosa with our authentic Pakistani Samosa recipe. Perfect as an appetizer for any occasion!
seo.h1: Authentic Pakistani Samosa Recipe: Crispy & Flavorful
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Pakistani Samosa
Pro Tips for the Best Crispy Samosa
Variations and Substitutions for Samosa Filling
seo.imageAlt: Plate of golden-brown, crispy authentic Pakistani Samosa with chutney, ready to serve as an appetizer.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy the irresistible crunch of homemade authentic Pakistani Samosa, a perfect appetizer for any gathering.

description: A crispy, savory pastry, Pakistani Samosa is a beloved appetizer featuring a delightful spiced potato and pea filling encased in a golden-brown, flaky crust. Perfect for tea time or any festive occasion, these triangular treats offer a burst of traditional flavors. Each bite delivers a satisfying crunch followed by a warm, aromatic interior that will transport your taste buds straight to the streets of Pakistan.

instructions.title: Prepare the Samosa Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine all-purpose flour, carom seeds, and salt. Add ghee or oil and rub it into the flour until it resembles breadcrumbs. Gradually add water, kneading until a firm, smooth dough forms. Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for at least 30 minutes. This resting period is crucial for a flaky crust.

instructions.title: Cook the Spiced Potato Filling
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a pan over medium heat. Add finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent. Stir in ginger-garlic paste and chopped green chilies, cooking for another minute until fragrant. Add cumin seeds, coriander powder, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Sauté the spices for a minute until aromatic.

instructions.title: Combine and Cool the Filling
instructions.text: Add the boiled and mashed potatoes along with the boiled green peas to the pan. Mix well, ensuring the spices are evenly distributed throughout the potato mixture. Cook for 3-5 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing the flavors to meld. Remove from heat and stir in the fresh chopped coriander. Transfer the filling to a separate bowl and let it cool completely. A cool filling prevents the dough from becoming soggy.

instructions.title: Shape and Fill the Samosas
instructions.text: Divide the rested dough into equal small balls, about the size of a golf ball. Roll each ball into an oval shape, then cut it in half to form two semi-circles. Take one semi-circle, moisten the straight edge with water, and fold it into a cone shape, sealing the seam tightly. Spoon 1-2 tablespoons of the cooled potato filling into the cone. Moisten the top edges of the cone with water and press them together to seal, forming the classic triangular samosa shape. Ensure a tight seal to prevent the filling from escaping during frying.

instructions.title: Fry the Samosas to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Heat vegetable oil in a deep karahi or frying pan over medium-low heat. The oil should be hot enough to sizzle when a tiny piece of dough is dropped in, but not smoking. Carefully place the shaped samosas into the hot oil, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Fry the samosas on medium-low heat, turning occasionally, for about 10-15 minutes or until they are golden brown and crispy. Frying on lower heat ensures the crust cooks thoroughly and becomes flaky.

instructions.title: Drain and Serve Hot
instructions.text: Once the samosas are golden brown and crispy, remove them from the oil using a slotted spoon. Place them on a wire rack or a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil. Serve the hot, crispy Pakistani Samosas immediately with your favorite mint chutney, tamarind chutney, or ketchup. They are a delightful snack or appetizer for any occasion.

tips: For the crispiest samosas, always fry them on medium-low heat. High heat will brown the exterior too quickly, leaving the dough raw inside.
Experiment with the filling by adding finely chopped paneer, minced chicken, or a mix of vegetables like carrots and beans for a different flavor profile.

Unfried samosas can be frozen for up to 2-3 months. Arrange them on a baking sheet to freeze individually, then transfer to an airtight container. Fry directly from frozen, adding a few extra minutes to the cooking time.
article: Samosa is a beloved Pakistani Appetizers dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
